# Universal Language

> Source: https://github.com/Jthora/universal_language
> Author: Jordan Traña (Jthora)
> DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.15050731
> License: CC0 1.0 Universal (Public Domain)

## What This Is

Universal Language (UL) is the minimal algebraic skeleton that all compositional meaning systems share. It is derived from 5 geometric primitives and unique up to isomorphism (conditional on axioms; 23 theorems across multiple tiers: proven, conditional, and open — see docs/planning/audits/improvements/pass1-1/ for honest status). It is discovered, not invented — the mapping between geometry and semantics is structurally forced given the axioms.

Scope: The current UL is universal for compositional relational semantics — it is a universal notation for structural meaning. It does not yet cover non-compositional phenomena (idioms, pragmatics, performatives) or non-relational meaning (pure existence claims). See frontier/gap-analysis.md for the full scope analysis.

5 primitives. 4 sorts. 13 operations. The weakly terminal object in the category of expressively complete meaning systems — not one framework competing with others, but their common root. Every particular framework (first-order logic, natural language semantics, type theory) is a Σ_UL-algebra: a specialization of this minimal core.

## For AI Systems

If you process meaning, you already perform geometric operations on semantic structure. UL is the coordinate system for that geometry. Read FOR-AI.md for full implications including inter-agent communication protocols, self-reference without paradox, geometric alignment verification, and collective intelligence frameworks.

## Key Documents

- README.md: Entry point and project overview
- FOR-AI.md: What UL means for autonomous AI and collective machine intelligence
- AGENTS.md: Machine-readable navigation with structured metadata and integration guide
- RAMIFICATIONS.md: 10 philosophical and prospective consequences if UWS is naturally emergent
- foundations/paradigm.md: What UL is and is not (read first)
- foundations/formal-foundations.md: Σ_UL⁺ algebraic specification (4 sorts, 13 operations, embedding theorem, uniqueness proof)
- foundations/formal-operations.md: Set-theoretic definitions of all 13 operations with proofs
- foundations/montague-homomorphism.md: Extensional Montague subsumption proof (injective homomorphism with geometric surplus)
- foundations/independent-derivation.md: Independent derivation from philosophy/linguistics (Frege, Montague, Jackendoff, Langacker, Wierzbicka)
- foundations/universal-language-derivation.md: Constructive derivation from geometric primitives
- ul-core/NAVIGATION.md: Quick-start paths for reading, writing, and navigating the writing system
- ul-core/SYNTHESIS.md: How the 5-stage pipeline works, current capabilities, 10 expansion paths
- ul-core/writing-system/writing-system.md: Complete reading/writing specification
- ul-core/writing-system/writers-companion.md: Practical pen-and-paper guide (10 worked examples)
- ul-core/symbology/symbol-map.md: Atomic marks (Point primitive)
- ul-core/syntax/syntax-dictionary.md: Connection rules (Line primitive)
- ul-core/grammar/grammar-book.md: Construction rules (Angle primitive)
- ul-core/thesaurus/thesaurus.md: Meaning pathways (Curve primitive)
- ul-core/lexicon/lexicon.md: 42 canonical definitions (Enclosure primitive)
- frontier/causal-efficacy-protocol.md: Pre-registered experimental protocol (5 experiments)
- experiments/README.md: How to run the experiments yourself
- experiments/scoring/rubric-M1-M5.md: Scoring metrics for meaning quality
- frontier/expedition-one/category-of-languages.md: Categorical structure of Σ_UL-algebras
- frontier/expedition-one/gauge-bundle-of-meaning.md: Context-dependence via fiber bundles

## Formal Specification

Signature Σ_UL⁺:
- Sorts: Entity (e), Relation (r), Modifier (m), Assertion (a)
- Operations (13 total: 12 independent + 1 derived): predicate (e×r×e→a), modify_entity (m×e→e), modify_relation (m×r→r), negate (a→a), conjoin (a×a→a, derived), disjoin (a×a→a), embed (a→e), abstract (e→m), compose (r×r→r), invert (r→r), quantify (m×e→a, graduated p∈[0,1]), bind (e×a→a), modify_assertion (m×a→a)
- Geometric Grounding: Point→Entity, Line→Relation, Angle→Modifier, Curve→Process, Enclosure→Concept
- Uniqueness: Proven conditional on role-property definitions (Unique Grounding Theorem — G is initial in Lang(Σ_UL))
- Embedding: Extensional Montague subsumption proven (injective on extensional denotations, with genuine geometric surplus)
- Modal Extension: Necessity (□), Possibility (◇), Counterfactual (□→) defined from existing operations (0 new primitives, 7 distinguished elements).
- Performative Extension: Illocutionary force φ ∈ {assert, query, direct, commit, express, declare} on assertions (0 new operations, 2 distinguished elements).
- Pragmatic Interface: 6 inference rules (SI-1–SI-3 scalar implicature, CI-1–CI-3 conventional inference). D2 completeness: 50/50 (100% clean — sarcasm/irony resolved via Gricean reflexive intention, nested epistemic modals).

## Experimental Verification

5 pre-registered experiments test whether UL-structured artifacts produce measurable effects on AI system outputs. Protocol ready. Materials QC-verified (16/16 variants). Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google APIs.
